patch.value: Fix per-type default_status config was silently ignored at create

pm schema add-type --default-status writes default_status into schema/types.json, but the read path dropped it: ItemTypeDefinition/ResolvedItemTypeDefinition had no field, normalizeItemTypeDefinition rebuilt the object without it, and create always used statusRegistry.open_status. A config-driven knob that did nothing — directly undermined the 'schema is config-driven, not hardcoded' goal. Threaded default_status through types.ts -> item-type-definition.ts -> type-registry.ts (coerce+apply) -> create.ts. Unknown values degrade to open_status (never-block).
Custom type with --default-status uses it on create when --status omitted; explicit --status overrides; invalid value degrades to open
Fixed: per-type default_status now honored at create; invalid degrades to open (never-block).

Threaded default_status end-to-end (types.ts, item-type-definition.ts, type-registry.ts coerce+apply, create.ts resolveCreateDefaultStatus). Verified live: Spike --default-status in_progress -> create uses in_progress; explicit --status overrides; invalid degrades to open. Tests: create-command.spec (integration) + item-type-definition.spec (normalize round-trip).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Bot-review follow-up (PR #86): completed default_status so it works on ALL config paths and modes. (1) Codex P2: added description+default_status to settings-validator itemTypeDefinition shape so INLINE settings.item_types.definitions are no longer stripped (verified: inline Spike default_status=in_progress applied at create). (2) CodeRabbit Major: requireCreateOptionByType now treats a configured default_status as satisfying a required-status policy in strict mode (scoped to default_status; types without a default still require explicit --status). (3) threaded description through ResolvedItemTypeDefinition for consistency. Added classifier + validator unit tests; 100% coverage holds.
